commands.review_summary:
Adds a draft RFC defining an optional ContextEngineRuntimeSettings payload for selected context-engine lifecycle hooks, including field semantics, invariants, and compatibility rules.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature/API RFC PR, not a runtime bug report. Source review of the RFC, README lifecycle, and linked implementation is the relevant check.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer RFC/API acceptance and cross-repo contract alignment, not an automated repair in this repository.

target.title: RFC: Context Engine Runtime Settings
target.body:
## Summary

This opens a draft RFC for `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings`, a small context-engine API contract that lets OpenClaw produce a per-invocation runtime snapshot for selected lifecycle hooks.

## Motivation

The related implementation PR needs an RFC because it changes the public context-engine/plugin SDK surface. The RFC is framed as a core OpenClaw API improvement for context-engine consumers, not as HyperMem-specific behavior.

## Linked context

- Related implementation PR: openclaw/openclaw#88750
- RFC status: `draft`
- Implementation issue: intentionally blank until the RFC is accepted
- Maintainer discussion thread: pending

## Authorship and assistance

Proposed by ragesaq. AI-assisted drafting and review by Forge/Codex.
target.author: ragesaq
